skills.name: Identifier
skills.desc: In Java, an identifier is a name given to a class, method, variable, or other program elements. It must follow specific naming rules and conventions.

skills.name: Data Types
skills.desc: Java supports various data types, including int, double, boolean, char, and more, which determine the kind of values that variables can hold.

skills.name: Operators
skills.desc: Operators in Java are symbols used to perform operations on variables and values. They include arithmetic, relational, logical, and assignment operators.

skills.name: Booleans
skills.desc: Booleans represent true or false values in Java. They are used in conditional expressions and control flow to make decisions.

skills.name: Variables
skills.desc: Variables in Java are used to store data temporarily. They have a data type, a name, and can hold different values during program execution.

skills.name: If...Else
skills.desc: The if...else statement in Java is used to make decisions in code. It allows you to execute different code blocks based on a specified condition.

skills.name: Switch
skills.desc: The switch statement in Java is used for multi-branch decision making. It evaluates an expression and executes the code block associated with the matching case.

skills.name: For Loop
skills.desc: The for loop in Java is used for repetitive execution of a block of code. It's commonly used for iterating over arrays and collections.

skills.name: While Loop
skills.desc: The while loop in Java repeatedly executes a block of code as long as a specified condition is true. It's used for general-purpose looping.

skills.name: Break/Continue
skills.desc: The break and continue statements in Java provide control flow manipulation within loops. Break terminates a loop, while continue skips the current iteration.

skills.name: Comments
skills.desc: Comments in Java are used for documentation and code readability. They are ignored by the compiler and are essential for explaining code logic.

skills.name: Output
skills.desc: Output in Java involves displaying information to the console or other output devices. It's achieved using methods like print and println.

skills.name: Type Casting
skills.desc: Type casting allows you to convert data from one data type to another. Java supports both implicit and explicit type casting.

skills.name: Math
skills.desc: Java provides a Math class with methods for performing mathematical operations, such as addition, subtraction, multiplication, and more.

skills.name: Strings
skills.desc: Strings are sequences of characters in Java and are used extensively in text processing and manipulation tasks.

skills.name: StringBuffer/StringBuilder
skills.desc: StringBuffer and StringBuilder classes in Java are used for efficient string manipulation, especially when there is a need for frequent modifications.

skills.name: RegEx
skills.desc: Regular expressions in Java enable pattern matching and text searching within strings. They are powerful tools for data validation and extraction.

skills.name: Arrays
skills.desc: Arrays are a fundamental data structure in Java, used for storing collections of elements of the same data type.

skills.name: Arrays Methods
skills.desc: Java provides various methods for working with arrays, including sorting, searching, and copying elements between arrays.

skills.name: Sorting
skills.desc: Sorting algorithms are essential for arranging data in a particular order. Java includes built-in methods for sorting arrays and collections.

skills.name: Collections Methods
skills.desc: Java's Collections framework provides a wide range of methods for working with collections, including lists, sets, and maps.

skills.name: Method Overloading
skills.desc: Method overloading allows you to define multiple methods with the same name but different parameter lists. It enhances code readability and reusability.

skills.name: Method Overriding
skills.desc: Method overriding in Java involves defining a method in a subclass with the same name, return type, and parameters as a method in its superclass.

skills.name: Recursion
skills.desc: Recursion is a programming technique where a method calls itself to solve problems that can be broken down into smaller, similar subproblems.

skills.name: Scope
skills.desc: Scope defines the visibility and lifetime of variables in Java. It includes local, instance, and class-level variable scopes.

skills.name: Lambda
skills.desc: Lambda expressions in Java enable the creation of anonymous functions or closures, simplifying code for functional-style programming.

skills.name: Classes/Objects
skills.desc: Classes and objects are fundamental concepts in Java. Classes serve as blueprints for creating objects, allowing you to define attributes and methods that characterize objects.

skills.name: Class Attributes
skills.desc: Class attributes are variables defined within a class that are shared by all instances of the class. They represent characteristics or properties common to all objects of that class.

skills.name: Class Methods
skills.desc: Class methods, also known as static methods, are methods associated with a class rather than an instance of the class. They are called on the class itself and are often used for utility functions.

skills.name: Constructors
skills.desc: Constructors are special methods in Java used to initialize object instances. They have the same name as the class and are invoked when objects are created.

skills.name: Modifiers
skills.desc: Modifiers in Java are keywords that specify access levels, scope, and behavior of classes, methods, and variables. They include public, private, protected, static, final, and more.

skills.name: Packages / API
skills.desc: Java packages provide a way to organize and structure code. The Java API (Application Programming Interface) includes predefined classes and libraries for common tasks.

skills.name: User Input
skills.desc: User input handling involves taking input from users through various input devices or methods, such as the keyboard, mouse, or command-line interface.

skills.name: Date
skills.desc: The Date class in Java is used to work with dates and times. It provides methods for parsing, formatting, and manipulating date and time values.

skills.name: OOP
skills.desc: Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) is a programming paradigm that emphasizes objects, classes, and their interactions to model real-world entities.

skills.name: Inheritance
skills.desc: Inheritance in Java allows a class to inherit properties and behaviors from another class. It promotes code reuse and hierarchy.

skills.name: Polymorphism
skills.desc: Polymorphism enables objects of different classes to be treated as objects of a common superclass. It facilitates flexibility and dynamic method invocation.

skills.name: Encapsulation
skills.desc: Encapsulation is the concept of bundling data and methods that operate on the data within a single unit, or class. It provides data hiding and access control.

skills.name: Abstraction
skills.desc: Abstraction simplifies complex systems by representing the essential features while hiding implementation details. It is achieved through classes, methods, and interfaces.

skills.name: Interface
skills.desc: Interfaces define a contract for classes to implement. They enable multiple inheritance-like behavior and are used to achieve abstraction and polymorphism.

skills.name: Enums
skills.desc: Enums are special data types in Java for defining a set of constants. They are commonly used to represent a fixed set of values or options.

skills.name: Exceptions
skills.desc: Exceptions in Java are used to handle runtime errors and exceptional conditions. They allow for graceful error recovery and control flow.

skills.name: Wrapper Classes
skills.desc: Wrapper classes in Java provide a way to convert primitive data types into objects. They are often used in collections and when working with object-oriented concepts.

skills.name: ArrayList
skills.desc: ArrayList is a dynamic array-like data structure in Java that allows for the storage of elements of the same data type. It provides dynamic resizing and convenient methods.

skills.name: LinkedList
skills.desc: LinkedList is a data structure in Java that consists of a sequence of elements, each linked to the next element. It is efficient for insertions and deletions.

skills.name: HashMap
skills.desc: HashMap is a key-value pair data structure in Java that provides efficient retrieval and storage of elements. It is widely used for data mapping and caching.

skills.name: HashSet
skills.desc: HashSet is an implementation of the Set interface in Java, which represents an unordered collection of unique elements. It is useful for maintaining unique values.

skills.name: Iterator
skills.desc: Iterators in Java provide a way to traverse collections, such as lists and sets, and access their elements sequentially. They simplify the process of iterating through data.

skills.name: Inner Classes
skills.desc: Inner classes are classes defined within other classes. They can access outer class members and provide a way to logically group related classes together.

skills.name: Annotation
skills.desc: Annotations in Java provide metadata about classes, methods, and other program elements. They are used for documentation, code analysis, and influencing runtime behavior.

skills.name: Generics
skills.desc: Generics enable type parameterization in Java, allowing you to create classes, interfaces, and methods that operate on specified data types. They enhance code reusability and type safety.

skills.name: Format
skills.desc: Formatting in Java involves the presentation of data in a specific, well-defined manner. It includes formatting dates, numbers, and text.

skills.name: Reflect
skills.desc: Reflection in Java allows you to inspect and manipulate classes, methods, fields, and other program elements at runtime. It is often used for frameworks and advanced debugging.

skills.name: Serialization
skills.desc: Serialization is the process of converting Java objects into a byte stream for storage or transmission. It plays a crucial role in persisting object state.

skills.name: JDBC
skills.desc: JDBC is a Java API that provides a standard interface for connecting Java applications to relational databases. It allows for executing SQL queries, fetching results, and managing database connections in a Java program.

skills.name: Files
skills.desc: Working with files is a common task in Java. This skill covers file operations like creation, writing, reading, and deletion.

skills.name: Create/Write Files
skills.desc: Java provides mechanisms for creating and writing data to files. This skill covers file creation, writing, and data serialization.

skills.name: Read Files
skills.desc: Reading data from files is a common operation in Java. This skill includes techniques for reading text and binary data from files.

skills.name: Delete Files
skills.desc: Deleting files and directories is part of file management. This skill covers methods for deleting files and handling exceptions.

skills.name: IO
skills.desc: Input/Output (IO) operations in Java involve reading and writing data to various sources, such as files, streams, and network connections.

skills.name: Stream
skills.desc: Streams in Java allow for efficient reading and writing of data. They are used for processing large volumes of data or working with files and network resources.

skills.name: NIO
skills.desc: Java NIO is a modern, non-blocking IO framework that offers improved performance and scalability for network and file IO operations.

skills.name: Threads
skills.desc: Threads in Java allow for concurrent execution of tasks. Understanding thread management is crucial for efficient and responsive applications.

skills.name: Working
skills.desc: This skill encompasses various programming techniques and best practices when working with Java, including code organization, debugging, and project management.

skills.name: Net
skills.desc: Networking in Java involves communication between applications over a network. This skill covers socket programming, client-server communication, and network protocols.

skills.name: XML/Dom4j
skills.desc: XML/Dom4j is used in Java for parsing and manipulating XML documents. Dom4j is a popular Java library for XML processing.

skills.name: Math Methods
skills.desc: The Math class in Java provides a set of methods for performing various mathematical operations, such as trigonometry, logarithms, and rounding.

skills.name: Object Methods
skills.desc: Object methods in Java, such as equals, hashCode, and toString, are inherited from the Object class and can be overridden to customize object behavior.

skills.name: String Methods
skills.desc: Java offers a wide range of methods for manipulating strings, including concatenation, substring extraction, searching, and text formatting.

skills.name: System Methods
skills.desc: System methods in Java provide access to system-level operations, such as reading environment variables, managing input and output streams, and more.
